note:
“Power Platform的伟大理念是，我们想让它成为人人都能访问的开发工具，”微软负责其低代码平台的企业副总裁Charles Lamanna告诉我。开发包括使用Power BI了解和报告您的数据，使用Power Apps构建Web和移动应用程序，使用Power Automate自动执行任务(无论是通过机器人流程自动化还是工作流自动化)，或者使用Power Virtual Agent构建聊天机器人和基于聊天的体验。
Power Automate已经允许用户连接基于网络的应用程序，类似于Zapier和IFTTT，但该公司去年年底也推出了一个浏览器扩展，以帮助用户将本地系统组件连接到Power Automate。现在，随着Softomotive技术的集成和这个新的低码Windows应用程序的推出，它将这种集成到本地Windows用户界面中又向前迈进了一步。
Lamanna解释说：“所有东西仍然在云中运行，仍然连接到云，但是现在你有了一个丰富的桌面应用程序来创作和记录你的UI自动化。”他将其比作“终极连接器”，并指出“终极API只是UI”。
他还强调，这款新应用给人的感觉和其他任何现代Office应用一样，比如Outlook(顺便说一句，今天将推出新的Mac版本)或Word。与这些应用程序的现代版本一样，Power Automate Desktop的很多功能都来自于连接到云。
同样值得注意的是，Power Automate不仅是一个用于自动执行简单的两步或三步流程的平台(例如，当您的老板给您发电子邮件时给您发送短信)，它还可以用于多步骤的关键业务工作流程。例如，T-Mobile正在使用该平台自动化其系统和Sprint之间的一些集成过程。
Lamanna指出，对于一些大型企业来说，采用这种低代码服务需要进行一些文化转变。毕竟，例如，它仍然需要对如何使用这些工具有一些洞察力，以确保数据的安全。
该公司今天宣布的另一个新功能是Power平台和GitHub之间的集成，目前正在进行公开预览。这里的想法是让开发人员能够创建他们自己的软件生命周期工作流。Lamanna说：“Power Platform的核心理念之一是它的代码很低。”“因此，它首先是为业务用户、业务分析师，而不是传统开发人员打造的。但是支持开发人员是受欢迎的。我的说法是：我们要为商业用户举办一个派对，但专业开发人员也会被邀请参加这个派对。但为了让他们进入平台，团队想要在他们所在的地方与他们见面，让他们使用他们已经使用的工具-那就是GitHub(以及Visual Studio和Visual Studio代码)。
note_en:
“The big idea of Power Platform is that we want to go make it so development is accessible to everybody,” Charles Lamanna, Microsoft’s corporate VP for its low-code platform, told me. “And development includes understanding and reporting on your data with Power BI, building web and mobile applications with Power Apps, automating your tasks — whether it’s through robotic process automation or workflow automation — with Power Automate, or building chatbots and chat-based experiences with Power Virtual Agent.”
 Power Automate already allowed users to connect web-based applications, similar to  Zapier   and IFTTT, but the company also launched a  browser extension earlier late last year to help users connect native system components to Power Automate. Now, with the integration of the Softomotive technology and the launch of this new low-code Windows application, it’s taking this integration into the native Windows user interface one step further.
   “Everything still runs in the cloud and still connects to the cloud, but you now have a rich desktop application to author and record your UI automations,” Lamanna explained. He likened it to an ‘ultimate connector,’ noting that the “ultimate API is just the UI.”
 He also stressed that the new app feels like any other modern Office app like Outlook (which is getting a new Mac version today, by the way) or Word. And like the modern versions of those apps, Power Automate Desktop derives a lot of its power from being connected to the cloud.
 It’s also worth noting that Power Automate isn’t just a platform for automating simple two- or three-step processes (like sending you a text message when your boss emails you), but also for multistep, business-critical workflows. T-Mobile, for example, is using the platform to automate some of the integration processes between its systems and Sprint.
 Lamanna noted that for some large enterprises, adopting these kinds of low-code services necessitates a bit of a culture shift. IT still needs to have some insights into how these tools are used, after all, to ensure that data is kept safe, for example.
 Another new feature the company announced today is an integration between the Power Platform and GitHub, which is now in public preview. The idea here is to give developers the ability to create their own software lifecycle workflows. “One of the core ideas of Power Platform is that it’s low code,” Lamanna said. “So it’s built first for business users, business analysts, not the classical developers. But pro devs are welcome. The saying I have is: we’re throwing a party for business users, but pro devs are also invited to the party.” But to get them onto the platform, the team wants to meet them where they are and let them use the tools they already use — and that’s GitHub (and Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code).
title: 微软为其Power平台带来新的机器人过程自动化功能
title_en: Microsoft brings new robotic process automation features to its Power Platform

note:
该公司特别将这些服务定位为使用地理空间数据的解决方案，并且已经与amergint、Kratos、KSAT、KubOS、Viasat、美国电子动力公司和Viasat合作，将服务推向市场。
Azure Networking首席产品经理Yves Pitsch在今天的博客中写道：“微软在收集、传输和处理地理空间数据方面能够很好地支持客户需求。”我们的智能云和边缘战略目前扩展到60多个已宣布的云区域、先进的分析和人工智能功能，再加上世界上速度最快、弹性最强的网络之一-安全和创新是我们所做的一切的核心。“。
这里的承诺是，卫星运营商不仅可以在微软的云上运行数据分析，还可以运行他们所有的数字地面操作。这包括在微软拥有和运营的地面站(使用X、S和UHF频率)安排与他们的航天器接触的能力。然后，这些数据可以立即流入Azure的各种解决方案，用于存储、分析和机器学习。
通过AWS地面站，亚马逊已经提供了类似的地面站即服务产品，该产品还包括全球天线网络和对AWS云的直接访问。不过，AWS更进一步，最近成立了一个专门的航空航天和卫星解决方案业务部门。
note_en:
The company is specifically positioning the services as a solution for working with geospatial data and itis already partnering with Amergint, Kratos, KSAT, KubOS, Viasat, US Electrodynamics and Viasat to bring the service to market.
   “Microsoft is well-positioned to support customer needs in gathering, transporting, and processing of geospatial data,” Yves Pitsch, Principal Product Manager, Azure Networking, writes in today’s blog post. “With our intelligent cloud and edge strategy currently extending over sixty announced cloud regions, advanced analytics, and AI capabilities coupled with one of the fastest and most resilient networks in the world – security and innovation are at the core of everything we do.”
 The promise here is that satellite operators will be able to run not just the data analysis on Microsoft’s cloud but all of their digital ground operations. That includes the ability to schedule contacts with their spacecraft over Microsoft’s owned and operated ground stations (using X, S and UHF frequencies). That data can then immediately flow into Azure’s various solutions for storage, analysis and machine learning.
 With  AWS Ground Stations, Amazon already offers a similar ground station-as-a-service product that also includes a global network of antennas and direct access to the  AWS   cloud. AWS went one step further, though, and recently  launched a dedicated business unit for aerospace and satellite solutions.
title: 微软发射Azure Orbary将卫星连接到其云
title_en: Microsoft launches Azure Orbital to connect satellites to its cloud

note:
正如微软所描述的，这里的想法是建立一个基本上可以作为天气监测系统的系统，但不是为了应对疾病爆发。该公司于2015年首次展示了该项目，但自那以来已经走过了相当长的一段路。
预感听起来像是一个相当疯狂的项目，但微软表示，它是基于这一领域五年的研发。该公司表示，它正在与美国国家科学基金会(National Science Foundation)的融合加速器计划(Conversion Accelerator Program)以及约翰·霍普金斯大学(Johns Hopkins University)、范德比尔特大学(Vanderbilt University)、匹兹堡大学(University Of Pittsburgh)和华盛顿大学(University Of Washington)的健康指标与评估研究所(Institute For Health Metrics And Evaluation)等学术合作伙伴合作，此外，该公司还与制药巨头拜耳(Bayer)合作，“加深对病媒传播疾病的理解，以及自主传感器网络在生物疗法检测中的作用。”
目前，人们的注意力似乎集中在蚊子传播的疾病上，微软实际上在其雷德蒙园区设立了一个“预感试验场”，帮助研究人员测试他们的机器人，训练他们的机器学习模型，并分析他们收集的数据。在这个节肢动物遏制二级设施中，该公司可以饲养和分析蚊子。但我们的想法是远远超越这一点，并监测整个生物群。
微软说，到目前为止，预感系统已经扫描了超过80万亿个碱基对的基因组材料，以寻找生物威胁。
“大约五年前，我们看到机器人、人工智能和云计算正在达到一个临界点，我们可以用全新的方式、以全新的规模监控生物群，”预感高级总监伊桑·杰克逊(Ethan Jackson)在该公司今天发布的一段视频中说。“真正让我们意识到这一点的是2014年的埃博拉疫情。地球上最罕见的病毒之一是如何从动物传染给人类而导致这次爆发的？我们错过了什么信号，可能会让我们预测到这一点呢？“。
两年后的2016年，当寨卡病毒出现时，该团队已经建立了一支小型智能机器人捕蚊器舰队，可以自动识别和捕获蚊子。该系统识别蚊子，然后可以瞬间决定是捕捉它还是让它飞。杰克逊说，在一个晚上，这个捕蚊器已经能够识别多达1万只蚊子。
在美国，微软首先部署这些系统的地方是德克萨斯州的哈里斯县。
“我们现在在蚊子治疗方面所做的一切都是反应性的--我们看到了很多蚊子，我们去喷洒了很多蚊子，”昆虫学家道格拉斯·E·诺里斯(Douglas E.Norris)说。他是约翰·霍普金斯大学分子微生物学和免疫学教授，也参与了这个项目。“想象一下，如果你有一个预测系统，根据所有这些数据和这些模型，几天后你会发现很多蚊子-然后你可以在蚊子叮咬、喷洒和攻击它们之前更早地出去治疗它们，这样你就不会得到那些可能导致疾病传播的大蚊子爆发。”
当然，这是一个非常雄心勃勃的项目。为什么微软现在要在它的Ignite大会上宣布呢？不出所料，整个系统依赖于Microsoft Azure云来提供运行所需的存储和计算能力--这也是微软展示其AI系统的一个很好的方式。
note_en:
The idea here, as Microsoft describes it, is to set up a system that can essentially function as a weather monitoring system, but for disease outbreaks. The company first demonstrated the project in 2015, but it has come quite a long way since.
 Premonition sounds like a pretty wild project, but Microsoft says it’s based on five years of R&D in this area. The company says it is partnering with the  National Science Foundation’s   Convergence Accelerator Program and academic partners like Johns Hopkins University,  Vanderbilt University,   the University of Pittsburgh and the University of Washington’s Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation to test the tools it’s developing here. In addition, it is also working with pharmaceutical giant Bayer to “develop a deeper understanding of vector-borne diseases and the role of autonomous sensor networks for biothreat detection.”
   Currently, it seems, focus is on diseases transmitted by mosquitos and Microsoft actually set up a ‘Premonition Proving Ground’ on its Redmon campus to help researchers test their robots, train their machine learning models and analyze the data they collect. In this Arthropod Containment Level 2 facility, the company can raise and analyze mosquitos. But the idea is to go well beyond this and monitor the entire biome.
 So far, Microsoft says, the Premonition system has scanned more than 80 trillion base-pairs of genomic material for biological threats.
 “About five years ago, we saw that robotics, AI and cloud computing were reaching a tipping point where we could monitor the biome in entirely new ways, at entirely new scales,” Ethan Jackson, the senior director of Premonition, said in a video the company released today. “It was really the 2014 Ebola outbreak that led to this realization. How did one of the rarest viruses on the planet jump from animal to people to cause this outbreak? What signals are we missing that might have allowed us to predict it?”
  Two years later, in 2016, when Zika emerged, the team had already built a small fleet of smart robotic traps that could autonomously identify and capture mosquito. The system identifies the mosquito and can then make a split-second decision whether to capture it or let it fly. In a single night, Jackson said, the trap has already been able to identify up to 10,000 mosquitos.
 In the U.S., the first place where Microsoft deployed these systems was Harris County, Texas.
  “Everything we do now in terms of mosquito treatment is reactive – we see a lot of mosquitoes, we go spray a lot of mosquitoes,” said Douglas E. Norris, an entomologist and Johns Hopkins University professor of molecular microbiology and immunology, who was part of this project. “Imagine if you had a forecasting system that shows, in a few days you’re going to have a lot of mosquitoes based on all this data and these models – then you could go out and treat them earlier before they’re biting, spray, hit them early so you don’t get those big mosquito blooms which then might result in disease transmission.”
 This is, by all means, a very ambitious project. Why is Microsoft announcing it now, at its Ignite conference? Unsurprisingly, the whole system relies on the Microsoft Azure cloud to provide the storage and compute power to run — and it’s a nice way for Microsoft to show off its AI systems, too.
title: 微软推出预感，这是其用于检测生物威胁的硬件和软件平台
title_en: Microsoft launches Premonition, its hardware and software platform for detecting biological threats

note:
今天的公告是在今年夏天早些时候推出了像一起模式和动态视图这样的功能之后发布的。
Together Mode将会议参与者的剪影放在不同的环境中，今天随着礼堂、咖啡厅和会议室等新场景的推出，它得到了一些更新。和以前一样，主持人选择场景，但现在的新情况是，微软现在也在使用机器学习来确保参与者自动集中在他们的虚拟椅子上，让整个场景看起来更自然一点(尽管微软的研究表明，我不禁觉得这看起来有点傻，可能是因为它让我想起了木偶秀的开场字幕)。
团队中的另一个新功能是自定义布局，它允许演示者自定义他们的演示文稿和他们自己的视频源的显示方式。利用这一点，例如，演示者可以将她自己的视频图像叠加到演示上。
突破室，这几天在Zoom中得到大量使用的一个功能，现在也进入了团队。微软称这是团队中要求最多的功能，就像在类似的产品中一样，它还可以让会议组织者将参与者分成更小的小组-然后会议组织者可以从一个房间走到另一个房间。不出所料，这一功能在教师中特别受欢迎，例如，公司也经常使用它来促进头脑风暴会议。
在这些分组会议室中用尽所有集思广益的力量并完成会议后，团队现在还可以向您发送会议的自动摘要，其中包括录音、文字记录、共享文件等。这些摘要将自动显示在您的Outlook日历上。未来，微软还将启用在SharePoint上自动存储这些录音的功能。
对于定期举办大型会议的公司，微软将在不久的将来推出对多达1000名参与者的支持。微软承诺，参加这些会议的人将获得完整的团队体验。稍后，微软还将允许多达20,000名参与者参加仅供查看的会议。这两个功能都将作为新的“高级通信”计划的一部分提供，考虑到管理1000人的会议可能需要多少带宽和计算能力，这可能并不令人惊讶。
微软今天还发布了两个与团队相关的硬件声明。首先是推出所谓的“微软团队面板”，这本质上是一种小型平板电脑，企业可以把它们放在会议室外用来指路。这里有一个很酷的特点-特别是当企业开始规划大流行后的办公室战略-例如，这些设备将能够使用房间摄像头的信息来计算有多少人亲自参加会议，然后显示剩余的房间容量。
该公司今天还宣布，巨型Surface Hub 2S 85英寸机型将于2021年1月上市。
而且还有更多。微软还为一线员工推出了新的团队功能，以帮助安排班次，提醒员工在下班时使用团队徽章，以及使组织能够识别员工的表扬徽章(尽管这些员工可能更喜欢硬通货而不是数字徽章)。
另外一个新功能是团队与RealWear头盔设备之间的远程协作集成，以及一个新的Android版Walkie Talkie应用程序。
由于数字徽章通常不足以提高员工的幸福感，微软还为团队增加了一套新的幸福感功能。这些网站为用户提供个性化的建议，帮助他们改变习惯，提高幸福感和生产力。
这包括一个新的“虚拟通勤”功能，包括与Headspace的集成和情感签到体验。
我一直喜欢短途和可管理的通勤方式，因为这样可以在上班和回家之间留出一定的距离，但现在这并不是一件真正的事情。也许Headspace可以作为一个例子，但我只能接受这么多安迪·普迪科姆(Andy Puddicombe)。不过，我认为我会对自己的情绪签到保密，尽管微软显然注意到它会将所有这些信息保密。
虽然企业现在关心的是你的情绪健康(因为它与你的生产力密切相关)，但经理们主要关心的是你完成的工作。对他们来说，Workplace Analytics正在进入团队，让“经理们了解团队工作规范，如下班后的协作、专注时间、会议效率和跨公司联系。然后，这些数据将与类似团队的平均水平进行比较，为经理们提供可操作的洞察力。“。
如果这都不能让你的经理高兴，还有什么会呢？也许是个数字表扬徽章？
note_en:
Today’s announcements follow the launch of features like  Together Mode and  dynamic view earlier this summer.
 Together Mode, which puts cutouts of meeting participants in different settings, is getting a bit of an update today with the launch of new scenes: auditoriums, coffee shops and conference rooms. Like before, the presenter chooses the scene, but what’s new now is that Microsoft is also now using machine learning to ensure that participants are automatically centered in their virtual chairs, making the whole scene look just a little bit more natural (and despite what Microsoft’s research shows, I can never help but think that this all looks a bit goofy, maybe because it reminds me of the opening credits of  the Muppet Show).
   Also new in Teams is custom layouts, which allow presenters to customize how their presentations — and their own video feeds — appear. With this, a presenter can superimpose her own video image over the presentation, for example.
  Breakout rooms, a feature that is getting a lot of use in Zoom these days, is now also coming to Teams. Microsoft calls it the most requested feature in Teams and like in similar products, it also meeting organizers to split participants into smaller groups — and the meeting organizer can then go from room to room. Unsurprisingly, this feature is especially popular with teachers, though companies, too, often use it to facilitate brainstorming sessions, for example.
  After exhausting all your brainstorming power in those breakout rooms and finishing up your meeting, Teams can now also send you an automatic recap of a meeting that includes a recording, transcript, shared files and more. These recaps will automatically appear on your Outlook calendar. In the future, Microsoft will also enable the ability to automatically store these recordings on SharePoint.
  For companies that regularly host large meetings, Microsoft will launch support for up to 1,000 participants in the near future. Attendees in these meetings will get the full Teams experience, Microsoft promises. Later, Microsoft will also enable view-only meetings for up to 20,000 participants. Both of these features will become available as part of a new ‘Advanced Communications’ plan, which is probably no surprise, given how much bandwidth and compute power it will likely take to manage a 1,000-person meetings.
  Microsoft also made two hardware announcements related to Team today. The first is the launch of what it calls ‘Microsoft Teams panels,’ which are essentially small tablets that businesses can put outside of their meeting rooms for wayfinding. One cool feature here — especially as business start planning their post-pandemic office strategy — is that these devices will be able to use information from the cameras in the room to count how many people are attending a meeting in person and then show remaining room capacity, for example.
 The company also today announced that the giant Surface Hub 2S 85-inch model will be available in January 2021.
 And there is more. Microsoft is also launching new Teams features for front-line workers to help schedule shifts, alert workers when they are using Teams off-shift and praise badges that enable organizations to recognize workers (though those workers would probably prefer hard cash over a digital badge).
 Also new is an integration between Teams and  RealWear head-mounted devices for remote collaboration and a new Walkie Talkie app for Android.
 And since digital badges aren’t usually enough to improve employee wellbeing, Microsoft is also adding a new set of wellbeing features to Teams. These provide users with personalized recommendations to help change habits and improve wellbeing and productivity.
  That includes a new ‘virtual commute’ feature that includes an integration with  Headspace and an emotional check-in experience.
 I’ve always been a fan of short and manageable commutes for getting some distance between work and home, but that’s not exactly a thing right now. Maybe Headspace works as an example, but there’s only so much Andy Puddicombe I can take. Still, I think I’ll keep my emotional check-ins to myself, though Microsoft obviously notes that it will keep all of that information private.
 And while businesses now care about your emotional wellbeing (because it’s closely related to your productivity), managers mostly care about the wor you get done. For them, Workplace Analytics is coming to Teams, giving “managers line of sight into teamwork norms like after-hours collaboration, focus time, meeting effectiveness, and cross-company connections. These will then be compared to averages among similar teams to provide managers with actionable insights.”
 If that doesn’t make your manager happy, what will? Maybe a digital praise badge?
title: 微软团队获得分组会议室、自定义布局和虚拟通勤
title_en: Microsoft Teams gets breakout rooms, custom layouts and virtual commutes

note:
其中包括Cortana在该公司与一些硬件供应商合作推出的新的微软团队显示器上的全面供应。你可以认为这些是团队的专用智能显示器，有点类似于谷歌助手的智能显示器，但只专注于会议。如今，如果不支持语音助手，就很难启用这样的设备，所以就这样吧。它将于9月份在美国推出英文版，然后在接下来的几个月里在澳大利亚、加拿大、英国和印度推出。
除了这些团队设备，微软并不一定将其定位于会议室，但作为常规笔记本电脑或台式机的助手，Cortana也将很快进入团队房间设备。一旦我们回到办公室和会议室，毕竟很少有人愿意触摸共享的硬件，所以非接触式体验是必须的。
一段时间以来，微软也一直在取笑更多以电子邮件为中心的Cortana服务。Play My Email是一项服务，可以大声朗读你的电子邮件，这项服务已经在美国的iOS和Android上提供，未来几个月将在澳大利亚、加拿大、英国和印度推出。但更重要的是，本月晚些时候，Outlook for iOS用户将能够通过语音与他们的收件箱互动，向电子邮件发件人发起呼叫，并播放来自特定发件人的电子邮件。
如果您是Microsoft 365企业用户，Cortana现在还可以向您发送每日简报电子邮件。此功能现已全面推出，并将在接下来的几个月中得到更好的会议准备、与微软待办事项的集成以及其他新功能。
例如，如果你在Windows10上使用Cortana，这款基于聊天的应用程序现在可以让你撰写电子邮件(至少如果你会说英语，而且在美国)。如果你愿意，你现在可以用唤醒词来启动它。
note_en:
These include the general availability of Cortana for the new  Microsoft Teams displays the company is launching in partnership with a number of hardware vendors. You can think of these as dedicated smart displays for Teams that are somewhat akin to Google Assistant-enabled smart displays, for example — but with the sole focus on meetings. These days, it’s hard to enable a device like this without support for a voice assistant, so there you go. It’ll be available in September in English in the U.S. and will then roll out to Australia, Canada, the UK and India in the coming months.
  In addition to these Teams devices, which Microsoft is not necessarily positioning for meeting rooms but as sidekicks to a regular laptop or desktop, Cortana will also soon come to Teams Rooms devices. Once we go back to offices and meeting rooms, after all, few people will want to touch a shared piece of hardware, so a touchless experience is a must.
  For a while now, Microsoft has also been teasing more email-centric Cortana services. Play My Emails, a service that reads you your email out aloud and that’s already available in the U.S. on iOS and  Android   is coming to n Australia,  Canada,   the UK and India in the coming months. But more importantly, later this month, Outlook for iOS users will be able to interact with their inbox by voice, initiate calls to email senders and play emails from specific senders.
 Cortana can now also send you daily briefing emails if you are a Microsoft 365 Enterprise users. This feature is now generally available and will get better meeting preparation, an integration with Microsoft To Do and other new features in the coming months.
 And if you’re using Cortana on Windows 10, this chat-based app now let you compose emails, for example (at least if you speak English and are in the U.S.). And if you so desire, you can now use a wake word to launch it.
title: 微软为商业用户推出新的Cortana功能
title_en: Microsoft launches new Cortana features for business users

note:
今天，该公司正在兑现这一承诺，推出了支持Azure Arc的数据服务预览版，如预期的那样，支持Azure SQL和Azure Database for PostgreSQL。
此外，微软正在提供Arc的核心功能，即支持Arc的服务器，这是普遍可用的。这些是该服务的核心工具，允许企业使用标准的Azure门户来管理和监控其多云和边缘环境中的Windows和Linux服务器。
微软Azure Data企业副总裁罗汉·库马尔(Rohan Kumar)表示：“我们一直都知道，企业正在寻求释放云的灵活性-他们喜欢应用程序模式，他们喜欢商业模式-同时平衡在内部维护某些应用程序和工作负载的需求，”微软Azure Data公司副总裁罗汉·库马尔(Rohan Kumar)表示。“很多客户实际上都有多云战略。在某些情况下，他们需要专门为合规性保留数据。在许多情况下，他们希望最大限度地利用现有投资。他们花了很多资本支出。“。
正如库马尔强调的那样，微软希望在客户所在的地方满足他们的需求，而不是强迫他们采用容器架构，或者更换他们专门的工程设备来使用Arc。
他说：“混合动力车实际上是为我们的客户提供灵活的选择，在他们所在的地方满足他们的需求，而不是开出解决方案。”
他承认这种方法使得设计解决方案变得更加困难，但是团队决定基线应该是容器端点，仅此而已。在很大程度上，微软打包了自己的工程师已经用来在公司自己的基础设施上运行Azure服务的工具，以便在多云环境中管理这些服务。
“事后看来，一开始有点挑战，因为，你可以想象，当我们最初建造它们的时候，我们没有想到我们会这样包装它们。但这是一个非常现代的设计点，“库马尔说。但结果是支持客户现在相对容易，因为它与团队在Azure中所做的工作非常相似。
Kumar指出，Azure数据服务的卖点之一也是Azure SQL的版本本质上是常青的，这让他们不用再担心SQL Server许可和生命周期结束支持的问题。
note_en:
Today, the company is making good on this promise with the preview launch of Azure  Arc   enabled data services with support for, as expected, Azure SQL and Azure Database for PostgreSQL.
 In addition, Microsoft is making the core feature of Arc, Arc enabled servers, generally available. These are the tools at the core of the service that allow enterprises can use the standard Azure Portal to manage and monitor their Windows and Linux servers across their multi-cloud and edge environments.
   “We’ve always known that enterprises are looking to unlock the agility of the cloud — they love the app model, they love the business model — while balancing a need to maintain certain applications and workloads on premises,” Rohan Kumar, Microsoft’s corporate VP for Azure Data said. “A lot of customers actually have a multi-cloud strategy. In some cases, they need to keep the data specifically for regulatory compliance. And in many cases, they want to maximize their existing investments. They’ve spent a lot of CapEx.”
 As Kumar stressed, Microsoft wants to meet customers where they are, without forcing them to adopt a container architecture, for example, or replace their specialized engineered appliances to use Arc.
 “Hybrid is really [about] providing that flexible choice to our customers, meeting them where they are, and not prescribing a solution,” he said.
 He admitted that this approach makes engineering the solution more difficult, but the team decided that the baseline should be a container endpoint and nothing more. And for the most part, Microsoft packaged up the tools its own engineers were already using to run Azure services on the company’s own infrastructure to manage these services in a multi-cloud environment.
 “In hindsight, it was a little challenging at the beginning, because, you can imagine, when we initially built them, we didn’t imagine that we’ll be packaging them like this. But it’s a very modern design point,” Kumar said. But the result is that supporting customers is now relatively easy because it’s so similar to what the team does in Azure, too.
 Kumar noted that one of the selling points for the Azure Data Services is also that the version of Azure SQL is essentially evergreen, allowing them to stop worrying about SQL Server licensing and end-of-life support questions.
title: 微软为其Arc多云管理服务带来数据服务
title_en: Microsoft brings data services to its Arc multi-cloud management service

note:
与竞争对手相比，Microsoft Azure为开发人员提供了访问更多数据中心区域的权限，但在这些区域为高可用性用例提供不同的可用区却为时已晚。在几年前几个备受瞩目的问题之后，它加快了构建可用区的路线图。目前，微软的12个地区都有可用区，正如该公司在Ignite大会上宣布的那样，加拿大中部和澳大利亚地区现在都将有可用区。
此外，该公司今天承诺，将在未来24个月内在其运营数据中心的每个国家推出可用区。
可用区的概念是为用户提供对位于相同地理区域但物理上独立的数据中心的访问，每个数据中心都有自己的电源、网络和连接基础设施。这样，如果其中一个数据中心因任何原因离线，同一地区还有另一个数据中心可以接管。
在早期，Microsoft Azure采取了略微不同的方法，将重点放在没有可用区的区域，认为地理扩展比提供区域更重要。谷歌采取了类似的方法，但它现在几乎为所有地区提供了三个可用区(爱荷华州提供了四个)。这里的总体想法是，开发人员总是可以为高可用性应用程序选择多个区域，但这仍然会带来额外的延迟，例如。
note_en:
Microsoft   Azure offers developers access to more data center regions than its competitors, but it was late to the game of offering different availability zones in those regions for high-availability use cases. After a few high-profile issues a  couple of years ago, it accelerated its roadmap for building availability zones. Currently, 12 of Microsoft’s regions feature availability zones and as the company announced at its Ignite conference, both the Canada Central and Australia region will feature availability zones now.
 In addition, the company today promised that it would launch availability zones in each country it operates data centers in within the next 24 months.
 The idea of an availability zone is to offer users access to data centers that in the same geographic region but are physically separate and each feature their own power, networking and connectivity infrastructure. That way, in case one of those data centers goes offline for whatever reason, there is still another one in the same area that can take over.
  In its early days, Microsoft Azure took a slightly different approach and focus on regions without availability zones, arguing that geographic expansion was more important than offering zones.  Google   took a somewhat similar approach, but it now offers three availability zones for virtually all of its regions (and four in Iowa). The general idea here was that developers could always choose multiple regions for high-availability applications, but that still introduces additional latencies, for example.
title: Microsoft Azure在加拿大和澳大利亚推出新的可用区
title_en: Microsoft Azure launches new availability zones in Canada and Australia

note:
该公司将新的一套服务描述为“主要云提供商提供的第一个完全托管的通信平台产品”，考虑到谷歌和AWS提供了其中一些功能，例如AWS通知服务，但不是作为聚合通信服务的一部分，这似乎是正确的。事实上，Azure通信服务似乎更像是Twilio或后起之秀MessageBird的核心功能的竞争对手。
在过去的几年中，微软在这一领域积累了大量经验，这在很大程度上决定了其团队服务的成功。不出所料，这也是微软在其声明中强调的。
“Azure通信服务本身就构建在全球可靠的云--Azure之上。企业可以自信地在微软团队每天支持5B+会议的低延迟全球通信网络上构建和部署，“该公司负责智能通信的公司副总裁Scott Van Vliet写道。
微软还强调，它提供了一套额外的智能服务，开发者可以利用这些服务来构建他们的通信服务，例如，包括它的翻译工具。该公司还指出，其服务经过加密，以满足HIPPA和GDPR标准。
与类似服务一样，开发人员通过一组新的API和SDK访问各种功能。
至于核心服务，这里的功能与您预期的差不多。有语音和视频通话(以及在它们之间切换的能力)。它支持聊天，从10月份开始，用户还可以发送短信。微软表示，随着微软将其定位为全球服务，开发者将能够将这些信息发送给任何地方的用户。
提供电话号码也是服务的一部分，开发人员将能够为入站和出站呼叫提供电话号码，移植现有号码，请求新号码，最重要的是，将它们与现有的内部设备和运营商网络集成在一起-对于联系中心用户来说是最重要的。
Van Vliet写道：“我们的目标是满足企业的需求，并提供解决方案，帮助他们在今天的市场中保持弹性，推动业务向前发展。”我们看到，丰富的通信体验(通过语音、视频、聊天和短信实现)将继续成为企业跨设备和平台与客户联系的不可或缺的一部分。
note_en:
The company describes the new set of services as the ” first fully managed communication platform offering from a major cloud provider” and that seems right, given that  Google   and AWS offer some of these features, including the  AWS notification service, for example, but not as part of a cohesive communication service. Indeed, it seems Azure Communication Service is more of a competitor to the core features of Twilio or up-and-coming MessageBird.
 Over the course of the last few years, Microsoft has built up a lot of experience in this area, in large parts things to the success of its Teams service. Unsurprisingly, that’s something Microsoft is also playing up in its announcement.
  “Azure Communication Services is built natively on top a global, reliable cloud — Azure. Businesses can confidently build and deploy on the same low latency global communication network used by Microsoft Teams to support 5B+ meeting minutes daily,” writes Scott Van Vliet, Corporate Vice President for Intelligent Communication at the company.
 Microsoft also stresses that it offers a set of additional smart services that developers can tap into to build out their communication services, including its translation tools, for example. The company also notes that its services are encrypted to meet HIPPA and GDPR standards.
 Like similar services, developer access the various capabilities through a set of new APIs and SDKs.
 As for the core services, the capabilities here are pretty much what you’d expect. There’s voice and video calling (and the ability to shift between them). There’s support for chat and starting in October, users will also be able to send text messages. Microsoft says developers will be able to send these to users anywhere, with Microsoft positioning it as a global service.
 Provisioning phone numbers, too, is part of the services and developers will be able to provision those for in-bound and out-bound calls, port existing numbers, request new ones and — most importantly for contact-center users — integrated them with existing on-premises equipment and carrier networks.
 “Our goal is to meet businesses where they are and provide solutions to help them be resilient and move their business forward in today’s market,” writes Van Vliet. “We see rich communication experiences – enabled by voice, video, chat, and SMS – continuing to be an integral part in how businesses connect with their customers across devices and platforms.”
title: 微软推出Azure通信服务挑战Twilio
title_en: Microsoft challenges Twilio with the launch of Azure Communication Services

note:
如今，从未接受过外部资金的Mailchip正在迈出自己转型的下一步，推出了一系列基于人工智能的工具，让小企业可以轻松获得与其规模较大的竞争对手现在使用的功能相同的功能。例如，这包括为购物者提供个性化的产品推荐，以及用于行为定位的预测工具，以查看哪些用户最有可能购买某种东西。但现在也有一个新的人工智能支持的工具来帮助企业主设计他们自己的视觉资产(部分基于它对Sawa的收购)，以及一个帮助他们写出更好的电子邮件主题行的工具。
还有一个新的工具可以帮助企业选择下一个最好的行动。它会查看服务汇总的所有数据，并为用户提供有关如何提高他们的电子邮件活动绩效的可行建议。
梅尔黑猩猩的创始首席执行官本·切斯特纳特告诉我：“来到这里的旅程大约在四年前就开始了。”“我们当时骑得很高。电子邮件为我们带来了令人惊叹的成就。一切看起来都很好。我有一个选择，我觉得我可以卖掉公司，赚很多钱。我收到了一些邀请。或者我可以直接滑行，老实说。我可以在电子邮件中成为英雄，保持简单，继续赚钱。或者我可以接受另一个非常艰难的挑战，那将是MailChimp的第二幕。老实说，我不知道那会是什么。老实说，那是四年前的事了，真的什么事情都有可能发生。“。
但在与包括时任数据分析主管约翰·福尔曼(John Foreman)在内的团队交谈后，切斯特纳特将公司带上了这条新的道路，进军营销自动化领域。他告诉我，部分原因是他注意到电子邮件空间变得越来越拥挤。“你知道这是怎么结束的。我是说，有这么多竞争者你不可能永远呆在那里。所以我知道我们必须升级我们的比赛，“他说。
“这对我们来说是一个巨大的转变，”切斯特纳特承认。“当时，我们必须善于为其他客户细分市场，比如电子商务客户和其他客户。这对我们来说也是新鲜事。对我们来说，这是各种各样的新学科。把这种变化强加给你的员工是非常非常艰难的。我不禁感激地回首往事，我的员工们愿意和我一起踏上这段旅程。他们实际上对我有信心，这次发布--这个秋季发布--对我来说是我们四年来所做工作的巅峰之作。“。
有一件事是有帮助的，那就是邮黑猩猩已经有了电子商务客户-正如切斯特纳特所指出的那样，他们正在将系统推向极限。切斯特纳特承认，就在几年前，梅尔黑猩猩的文化还认为它们有点恼人，因为它们要求很高。他们甚至也没有给公司赚很多钱。当时，非营利组织是梅尔黑猩猩最好的客户，但他们并没有将这项技术推向极限。
尽管进行了这一转变，但邮黑猩猩并没有进行大量收购来加速这一进程。切斯特纳特辩称，它正在做的很多事情-比如增加直邮-或多或少是它已经擅长的东西的延伸。但它确实进行了一些小规模的AI和ML收购，以带来正确的内部专业知识，以及包括LemonStand在内的两笔电子商务收购。最近，邮猩猩收购了英国杂志、时事通讯和播客Courier，标志着它第一次进军印刷业。
有了这套新的产品和服务，Mailchip现在的目标是让小企业获得大型电商长期以来拥有的相同功能，但不会那么复杂。
要构建基于机器学习的工具，人们需要数据--而这正是邮猩猩已经拥有的。
“我们做市场营销已经有几十年了，”邮猩猩CPO福尔曼说。“我们在这个平台上有数以百万计的小企业。因此，我们不仅自己建造了所有这些工具，这使得我们能够从视觉设计的角度将它们整合起来-它们不一定是收购-而且我们拥有多年在数百万家企业和我们正在交谈的数十亿客户进行营销的通用数据集，所以我们想，我们如何才能利用智能-人工智能、机器学习等-来逐步了解所有这些工具是如何联系在一起的。“。
切斯特纳特表示，他不太可能在短期内让公司进行类似的转型。“我真的相信你每十年只能进行一次重大转变，”他说。“所以你最好选对了，最好投资。我们都在这个支持电子商务的一体化营销平台上。这已经够独特的了。现在，我试图让我的公司做的是深入研究。“
note_en:
Today,  Mailchimp,   which has never taken outside funding, is taking the next step in its own transformation with the launch of a set of AI-based tools that give small businesses easy access to the same kind of capabilities that their larger competitors now use. That includes personalized product recommendations for shoppers and forecasting tools for behavioral targeting to see which users are most likely to buy something, for example. But there’s now also a new AI-backed tool to help business owners design their own visual asset (based in part on its  acquisition of Sawa), as well as a tool to help them write better email subject lines.
  There’s also a new tool that helps businesses choose the next best action. It looks at all of the data the service aggregates and gives users actionable recommendations for how to improve their email campaign performance.
  “The journey to get here started about four years ago,” Mailchimp’s founding CEO  Ben Chestnut told   me. “We were riding high. Email was doing amazing for us. And things look so good. And I had a choice, I felt I could sell the business and make a lot of money. I had some offers. Or I could just coast, honestly. I could just be a hero in email and keep it simple and just keep raking in the money. Or I could take on another really tough challenge, which would be act two of  MailChimp. And I honestly didn’t know what that would be. To be honest with you, that was four years ago, it could have been anything really.”
 But after talking to the team, including John Foreman, the head of data analytics at the time and now Mailchimp’s CPO, Chestnut put the company on this new path to go after the marketing automation space. In part, he told me, he did so because he noted that the email space was getting increasingly crowded. “You know how that ends. I mean, you can’t stay there forever with this many competitors. So I knew that we had to up our game,” he said.
   “It was a huge transformation for us,” Chestnut acknowledged. “We had to get good at building for other customer segments at the time, like e-commerce customers and others. And that was new for us, too. It’s all kinds of new disciplines for us. To inflict that kind of change on your employees is very, very rough. I just can’t help but look back with gratitude that my employees were willing to go on this journey with me. And they actually had faith in me and this release — this fall release — is really the culmination of everything we’ve been working on for four years to me.”
 One thing that helped was that Mailchimp already had e-commerce customers — and as Chestnut noted, they were pushing the system to its limit. Only a few years ago, the culture at Mailchimp looked at them as somewhat annoying, though, Chestnut admitted, because they were quite demanding. They didn’t even make the company a lot of money either. At the time, non-profits were Mailchimp’s best customers, but they weren’t pushing the technology to its limits.
  Despite this transformation, Mailchimp hasn’t made a lot of acquisitions to accelerate this process. Chestnut argues that a lot of what it is doing — say adding direct mail — is something that was more or less and extension of what it was already good at. But it did make some small AI and ML acquisitions to bring the right expertise in-house, as well as two e-commerce acquisitions, including  Lemonstand. Most recently, Mailchimp  acquired Courier, a British magazine, newsletter and podcast, marking its first move into the print business.
 With this new set of products and services, Mailchimp is now aiming to give small businesses access to the same capabilities the larger e-commerce players have long had, but without the complexity.
 To build tools based on machine learning, one needs data — and that’s something Mailchimp already had.
 “We’ve been doing marketing for decades,” Mailchimp CPO Foreman said. “And we have millions of small businesses on the platform. And so not only do we build all these tools ourselves, which allows us to integrate them from a visual design perspective — they’re not necessarily acquisitions — but we have this common data set from years and years of doing marketing across millions of businesses, billions of customers we’re talking to, and so we thought, how can we use intelligence — artificial intelligence, machine learning, etc. — to also sand down how all of these tools connect.”
 Chestnut says he isn’t likely to put the company on a similar transformation anytime soon. “I really believe you can only take on one major transformation per decade,” he said. “And so you better pick the right one and you better invest it. We’re all in on this all-in-one marketing platform that’s e-commerce enabled. That is unique enough. And now what I’m trying to get my company to do is go deep.”
title: MailChimp推出新的人工智能工具，继续向营销平台转型
title_en: Mailchimp launches new AI tools as it continues its transformation to marketing platform

三星发布了下一款高性能NVMe 2280大小的M.2硬盘，980 Pro。到目前为止，它在本月推出了三种容量：250 GB的售价为89.99美元，500 GB的售价为149.99美元，以及1TB的售价为229.99美元。一款2TB的机型将在今年晚些时候上市，但三星没有透露价格。
该硬盘的突出特点是与PCIe 4.0接口上的M.2插槽兼容。三星表示，如果你有兼容的主板，980Pro的顺序读写速度可以分别高达7000MB/s和5000MB/s。该公司声称，这比PCIe 3.0固态硬盘的性能快两倍，比价格更实惠但速度更慢的SATA固态硬盘快近13倍。
当然，为了让这款三星M.2硬盘达到最佳速度，你需要一块兼容的主板，带有PCIe4.0M.2插槽。这项技术的采用正在开始加速，包括主流计算产品，如AMD的第三代Ryzen CPU，其Radeon RX 5700和5700XT GPU，以及最近NVIDIA的RTX 3080显卡。索尼和微软也在PS5和Xbox系列S/X游戏机中使用这项技术来定制SSD技术。
考虑到它的相对婴儿期，大多数有足够知识去建造一台PC的人很有可能还没有一块支持PCIe4.0的主板。不过，解锁这款固态硬盘的潜力足以证明，或许应该进行升级了。
三星称，980 Pro连接到一个带有PCIe 3.0的M.2插槽上(如果主板有M.2插槽，大多数主板都支持这个接口)，对于1TB型号，980 Pro的顺序读取速度可以达到3,500MB/s，写入速度最高可以达到3,450MB/s。容量的每一级降低都有相同的读取速度要求，但写入速度略低：500 GB型号为3,400MB/s，250 GB型号为2,700MB/s。根据How-to Geek上的一篇帖子，写入速度的差异可能是由于较小的驱动器中存在的NAND通道较少，导致写入性能较慢。相反的情况被认为适用于较大的驱动器，以实现更好的性能。
令人鼓舞的是，即使安装在不是最尖端的主板上，新的980 Pro写入文件的速度也比970 Pro快，970 Pro的顺序读写速度分别为3500MB/s和2700MB/s(这是1TB的970Plus所宣称的；512 GB型号的读取速度为2300MB/s)。更好的是，三星的980Pro价格更实惠，存储空间也更大。
note_en:
If you buy something from a Verge link, Vox Media may earn a commission. See our  ethics statement.
 Samsung has unveiled its next high-performance NVMe 2280-sized M.2 drive, the 980 Pro. So far, it comes in three capacities shipping this month:  250GB for $89.99, 500GB for $149.99, and 1TB for $229.99. A 2TB model will arrive later this year, but Samsung didn’t share a price.
 The standout feature of this drive is its compatibility with M.2 slots over the PCIe 4.0 interface. If you have a compatible motherboard, Samsung says the 980 Pro can go on a tear with sequential read / write speeds of up to 7,000MB/s and 5,000MB/s, respectively. It claims that this is two times faster performance than PCIe 3.0 SSDs and nearly 13 times faster than the more affordable but slower SATA SSDs.
   Of course, to get the best speeds out of this Samsung M.2 drive, you’ll need a compatible motherboard with a PCIe 4.0 M.2 slot. Adoption of the tech is starting to ramp up, including mainstream computing products like  AMD’s third-generation Ryzen CPUs, its  Radeon RX 5700 and 5700XT GPUs, and more recently,  Nvidia’s RTX 3080 graphics card. Sony and Microsoft are also using the technology  for their custom SSD technologies in the PS5 and Xbox Series S / X consoles.
 Given its relative infancy, the chances are high that most people savvy enough to build a PC don’t yet have a PCIe 4.0-ready motherboard. Though, unlocking this SSD’s potential is good enough evidence that maybe an upgrade should be in order.
   Attached to a M.2 slot with PCIe 3.0 (the interface that most of today’s motherboards support, if they have an M.2 slot), Samsung says the 980 Pro can reach sequential read speeds of 3,500MB/s and up to 3,450MB/s write speeds for the 1TB model. Each step down in capacity has the same read speed claims but with slightly slower write speeds: 3,400MB/s for the 500GB model and 2,700MB/s for the 250GB version.  According to a post on  How-to Geek, the write speed differences might be due to fewer NAND channels present in a smaller drive, resulting in slower write performance. The inverse is supposedly true of larger drives to allow better performance.
 It’s encouraging that, even installed onto a motherboard that’s not on the bleeding edge, the new 980 Pro is faster at writing files than the 970 Pro, which was capable of sequential read / write speeds of 3,500MB/s and 2,700MB/s (these are the claims for the 970 Plus with 1TB; the 512GB model reads at 2,300MB/s). What’s better is that Samsung’s 980 Pro is more affordable and comes in more storage sizes.
tags: 固态硬盘,硬盘,支持,fast,速度
title: 三星支持PCIe 4.0的快速980 Pro固态硬盘可以让您的PC版本经得起未来考验
title_en: Samsung's Fast, PCIe 4.0-ready 980 Pro SSD Can Future-Proof Your PC Build

巴黎住房官员对欧洲法院(European Court Of Justice)的一项裁决表示欢迎，该裁决支持一项法国法律，该法律要求短期房产租赁必须获得当地授权。
其他面临长期租赁住房短缺的欧洲城市也在关注这一事件。
短期旅行专家Airbnb表示，它欢迎这一决定，该决定将有助于澄清规则。
Airbnb指出，它不是本案的当事人，并补充说，这项裁决涉及的是第二套住房，而不是人们的主要住所，因此，它表示，对巴黎的影响很小，甚至没有影响。
在欧洲法院做出裁决的几天前，来自欧洲大陆22个城市的代表呼吁欧盟官员制定规则，保护当地房地产市场免受短期假日租金的影响。
2017年5月，巴黎的两家旅游租赁公司Cali Apartments SCI和HX因未经巴黎当局授权出租两套单间公寓(通过Airbnb平台)而被罚款15,000欧元(13,600 GB；17,400美元)。
他们还被告知将公寓恢复为住宅用途，理由是住房法规定，在人口超过20万的城市和巴黎的三个地区，任何改变用途都需要获得许可。
这些公司对裁决提出上诉，案件被提交给卢森堡的欧洲法院。
法国法律的目的是与长期租赁住房短缺作斗争，并构成与公共利益相关的压倒一切的理由。
巴黎负责住房的最高官员伊恩·布罗萨特(Ian Brossat)宣布，这项裁决是一场巨大的胜利。市长安妮·伊达尔戈(Anne Hidalgo)表示，这项裁决支持法国关于Airbnb租赁规则的法律。
市长在推特上表示，许多城市都在等待这场胜利，这标志着季节性租金监管的一个转折点，也是向所有人的住房权迈出的一步。
Airbnb在一份声明中明确表示，它认为这项裁决仅限于在巴黎共用第二套住房的房东，并强调，大多数将自己的房产放在巴黎平台上的人都是共用他们的主要住所的。
它补充说，我们期待着与地方当局密切合作，制定比例监管，将当地家庭和社区放在首位，为所有人服务。
上周四，巴黎和包括巴塞罗那、佛罗伦萨、阿姆斯特丹和柏林在内的其他21个城市呼吁欧盟竞争事务专员玛格丽特·维斯特格(Margrethe Vestager)推动整个欧盟范围内的短期租赁规则框架，而不是现有的城市实施自己规则的体系。
2019年12月，法国酒店业试图迫使Airbnb被视为一家房地产租赁公司，但遭到了欧洲法院的拒绝，欧洲法院表示，这家互联网巨头是一家信息社会服务公司。
note_en:
Paris housing officials have welcomed a European Court of Justice ruling backing a French law that requires local authorisation for short-term property rentals.
  It was also being watched by other European cities facing a shortage of housing for longer-term rent.
 Short-stay specialist Airbnb said it welcomed a decision that "will go to help in clarifying the rules".
 Pointing out that it was not party to the case, Airbnb added that the ruling related to second homes and not people's primary residences, so would, it said, have little or no impact in Paris.
 The European Court ruling comes days after representatives from 22 cities across the continent called on European Union officials to come up with rules to protect local housing markets from short-term holiday rentals.
  In May 2017, two tourism rental companies in Paris, Cali Apartments SCI and HX, were fined €15,000 (£13,600; $17,400) for renting out two studio flats (via the Airbnb platform) without authorisation from Paris authorities.
 They were also told to restore the flats to residential use, citing a housing code that requires permission for any change of use in cities of over 200,000 residents and three areas of Paris.
 The firms appealed against the ruling and the case was referred to the European Court in Luxembourg.
   the aim of the French law was to combat "the long-term rental housing shortage" and "constitutes an overriding reason relating to the public interest"
  The top Paris official in charge of housing, Ian Brossat, declared the ruling a "fine victory". Mayor Anne Hidalgo said the ruling had backed French law on rules for Airbnb rentals.
  "This victory, awaited by many cities, marks a turning point for the supervision of seasonal rentals and constitutes a step forward for the right to housing for all,"  the mayor said on Twitter.
 In a statement, Airbnb made clear it saw the ruling as limited to "hosts who share secondary homes in Paris", emphasising that most people who put their properties on the platform in the French capital shared their main residence.
 "We look forward to working closely with local authorities on proportionate regulation that puts local families and communities first and works for all," it added.
  Last Thursday, Paris and 21 other cities including Barcelona, Florence, Amsterdam and Berlin called on EU competition commissioner Margrethe Vestager to push forward an EU-wide rule framework for short-term rentals, rather than the existing system in which cities impose their own rules.
 In December 2019, an attempt by France's hotel industry to force Airbnb to be seen as a property rental firm was rejected by the ECJ, which said instead that the internet giant was an "information society service".
title: 法国Airbnb：巴黎欢呼战胜短期租金
title_en: France Airbnb: Paris hails victory over short-stay rents

Facebook表示，由于影响其如何将数据从欧盟传输到美国的新规定，该公司可能会停止在欧洲运营其核心应用程序和Instagram。
该公司于9月10日向爱尔兰高等法院提交了一份宣誓书，爱尔兰“商业邮报”(Business Post)周日率先报道了这份文件。
在宣誓书中，Facebook挑战了爱尔兰数据隐私监管机构数据保护专员(DPC)本月早些时候发布的一项初步命令，该命令威胁要阻止Facebook将欧盟数据传输回美国，原因是担心隐私问题。
Facebook爱尔兰的数据保护主管兼副总法律顾问伊冯娜·坎纳内(Yvonne Cunnane)写道，如果申请人[Facebook]像DPC提议的那样，被完全暂停将用户数据转移到美国，申请人不清楚在这种情况下，它如何继续在欧盟提供Facebook和Instagram服务，并补充说，Facebook在欧洲拥有4.1亿月度活跃用户。
Facebook并没有威胁要退出欧洲。Facebook发言人在一份声明中表示，提交给爱尔兰高等法院的法律文件列出了一个简单的现实，Facebook和其他许多企业、组织和服务都依赖欧盟和美国之间的数据传输来运营他们的服务。
他补充说，缺乏安全、安全和合法的国际数据传输将损害经济，并阻碍欧盟数据驱动型企业的增长，就像我们寻求从新冠肺炎那里复苏一样。
获得了Facebook宣誓书完整版的副新闻报道称，这家科技巨头还抱怨说，它受到了不公平的对待，因为没有其他美国科技公司成为DPC的类似目标。
DPC的初步命令质疑Facebook目前用来将数据发回美国的系统，即标准合同条款(SCCS)，在向美国发送数据的情况下是否具有法律效力。
在欧盟废除了一项名为隐私盾牌(Privacy Shield)的长期欧盟-美国数据传输协议后，SCCS在7月份被认为总体上是有效的跨国数据传输机制。然而，要符合SCC的资格，各国必须表现出足够高的数据隐私水平，令人担忧的是，美国的数据监控过于侵入性，无法与欧盟在隐私方面的高标准相匹敌。

note_en:
Facebook says it may stop operating its core app and Instagram in Europe, thanks to new regulations impacting how it transfers data from the EU to the US.
 The company filed an affidavit to Ireland's high court on September 10, with Ireland's  Business Post being the first to report on the document on Sunday.
 In the affidavit, Facebook challenged a preliminary order issued by Ireland's data privacy watchdog the Data Protection Commissioner (DPC) earlier this month, which  threatened to block Facebook from transferring EU data back to the US over privacy concerns.
 "In the event that the Applicant [Facebook] were subject to a complete suspension of the transfer of users' data to the US, as appears to be what the DPC proposes, it is not clear to the Applicant how, in those circumstances, it could continue to provide the Facebook and Instagram services in the EU," wrote Facebook Ireland's head of data protection and associate general counsel Yvonne Cunnane, adding that Facebook has 410 million monthly active users in Europe.
    "Facebook is not threatening to withdraw from Europe. Legal documents filed with the Irish High Court set out the simple reality that Facebook, and many other businesses, organizations, and services, rely on data transfers between the EU and the US in order to operate their services," Facebook's spokesman said in a statement.
 "A lack of safe, secure, and legal international data transfers would damage the economy and hamper the growth of data-driven businesses in the EU, just as we seek a recovery from COVID-19," he added.
 Vice News, which obtained a full version of Facebook's affidavit, reported the tech giant also complained it was being treated unfairly, as no other US tech companies had been similarly targeted by the DPC.
   The DPC's preliminary order called into question whether the system Facebook currently uses to send data back to the US, called Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) is legally valid in the case of sending data to the US.
 SCCs were deemed valid overall as a cross-country data transfer mechanism in July, after the EU dismantled a long-standing EU-US data transfer agreement called Privacy Shield. However to qualify for SCCs, countries have to demonstrate a sufficiently high level of data privacy, and the concern is that US data surveillance is too invasive to match up with the EU's high standards on privacy.

俄罗斯政府正在努力更新其技术法，以便禁止使用可能阻碍其监控和审查能力的现代互联网协议。
根据一份最新拟议的法律修正案副本和一份政府解释说明，这项禁令针对的是互联网协议和技术，如TLS 1.3、DOH、DOT和ESNI。
莫斯科官员并不打算全面禁止HTTPS和加密通信，因为它们对现代金融交易、通信、军事和关键基础设施至关重要。
相反，政府希望禁止使用在HTTPS流量中隐藏网页名称(标识符)的互联网协议。
今天，虽然HTTPS对互联网连接的内容进行加密，但电信公司等第三方可以应用和确定用户连接到哪个站点的各种技术。
第三方可能无法破解加密和嗅探流量，但他们可以根据这些泄密来跟踪或阻止用户，这就是当今一些ISP级别的父母控制和版权侵权黑名单的运作方式。
电信公司目前使用的主要两种技术包括(1)监视DNS流量或(2)分析HTTPS流量中的SNI(服务器名称标识)字段。
第一种技术之所以有效，是因为浏览器和应用程序以明文形式进行DNS查询，甚至在未来的HTTPS连接建立之前就会显示用户预期的站点目的地。
第二种技术之所以有效，是因为HTTPS连接中的SNI字段未加密，类似地允许第三方确定HTTPS连接要去往哪个站点。
但在过去的十年里，已经创建并发布了新的互联网协议来解决这两个问题。
当组合使用TLS 1.3和ESNI(服务器名称标识)时，还可以防止SNI泄漏。
无论是在浏览器中，还是在全球的云提供商和网站中，这些协议都在慢慢获得采用，没有什么更好的迹象表明，这些新协议的工作效果与中国更新其长城防火墙审查工具来阻止依赖TLS 1.3和ESNI的HTTPS流量一样好。
俄罗斯不使用国家防火墙系统，但莫斯科政权依赖于一种名为SSORM的系统，该系统允许执法部门出于执法目的，在电信数据中心从源头拦截互联网流量。
此外，俄罗斯电信部Roskomnadzor通过其对当地互联网服务提供商的监管权力，一直在运行事实上的国家防火墙。在过去的十年里，Roskomnadzor一直在禁止它认为危险的网站，并要求ISP过滤他们的流量，阻止对各自网站的访问。
随着TLS 1.3、DOH、DOT和ESNI的采用，俄罗斯目前的所有监视和审查工具都将变得毫无用处，因为它们依赖于访问从加密的网络流量中泄露的网站标识符。
和中国一样，俄罗斯也在打击这些新技术。根据拟议的法律修正案，任何利用技术在加密流量中隐藏网站标识符的公司或网站，在发出一天警告后，将在俄罗斯境内被禁止。
拟议的法律目前正在进行公开辩论，等待公众反馈，直到下个月，也就是10月5日。
考虑到这项法律修正案带来的战略、政治和情报利益，几乎可以肯定修正案会获得通过。
note_en:
The Russian government is working on updating its technology laws so it can ban the use of modern internet protocols that can hinder its surveillance and censorship capabilities.
 According to a copy of the  proposed law amendments and an  explanatory note, the ban targets internet protocols and technologies such as  TLS 1.3,  DoH,  DoT, and  ESNI.
 Moscow officials aren't looking to ban HTTPS and encrypted communications as a whole, as these are essential to modern-day financial transactions, communications, military, and critical infrastructure.
 Instead, the government wants to ban the use of internet protocols that hide " the name (identifier) of a web page" inside HTTPS traffic.
  Today, while HTTPS encrypts the content of an internet connection, there are various techniques that third-parties such as telcos can apply and determine to what site a user is connecting.
 Third-parties may not be able to break the encryption and sniff on the traffic, but they can track or block users based on these leaks, and this is how some ISP-level parental control and copyright infringement blocklists work today.
 The primary two techniques used by telcos today include (1) watching DNS traffic or (2) analyzing the SNI (Server Name Identification) field in HTTPS traffic.
   The first technique works because browsers and apps make DNS queries in plaintext, revealing the user's intended site destination even before a future HTTPS connection is established.
 The second technique works because the SNI field in HTTPS connections is left unencrypted and similarly allows third-parties to determine to what site an HTTPS connection is going.
  But over the past decade, new internet protocols have been created and released to address these two issues.
  And when combined, TLS 1.3 and ESNI (Server Name Identification) can also prevent SNI leaks.
 These protocols are slowly gaining adoption, both in browsers and with cloud providers and websites across the globe, and there is no better sign that these new protocols work as advertised as the fact that  China updated its Great Firewall censorship tool to block HTTPS traffic that relied on TLS 1.3 and ESNI.
 Russia doesn't use a national firewall system, but the Moscow regime relies on a system called  SORM that allows law enforcement to intercept internet traffic for law enforcement purposes right at the source, in telco data centers.
 Furthermore, Russia's telecommunications ministry, the Roskomnadzor, has been running a de-facto national firewall through its regulatory power over the local ISPs. For the past decade, Roskomnadzor has been banning websites it deemed dangerous and asking ISPs to filter their traffic and block access to the respective sites.
 With TLS 1.3, DoH, DoT, and ESNI gaining adoption, all of Russia's current surveillance and censorship tools will become useless, as they rely on having access to the website identifiers that leak from encrypted web traffic.
 And just like China, Russia is cracking down on these new technologies. According to the proposed law amendment, any company or website that uses technology to hide its website identifier in encrypted traffic will be banned inside Russia after a one-day warning.
 The proposed law is currently in open debate and  awaiting public feedback until next month, on October 5.
 Taking into account the strategic, political, and intelligence benefits that come with this law amendment, it's almost certain that the amendment will pass.
title: 俄罗斯希望禁止使用TLS 1.3、DoH、DOT、ESNI等安全协议
title_en: Russia wants to ban the use of secure protocols such as TLS 1.3, DoH, DoT, ESNI

在今天的电池日活动上，特斯拉预计将发布新技术，这些技术将在未来十年推动该公司的产品。在这次活动之前，我们认为读者会喜欢这篇5月份首次发表的文章，这篇文章讲述了特斯拉在过去十年电池成本下降中所扮演的角色。
2010年，一个容量为1千瓦时的锂离子电池组-足以为一辆电动汽车提供三四英里的动力-的价格超过了1000美元。彭博社(BloombergNEF)汇编的数据显示，到2019年，这一数字已降至156美元。这是一个巨大的下降，专家们预计未来十年会继续-尽管可能不会那么快-进步。几位预测人士预计，到本世纪20年代中期，锂离子电池容量每千瓦时的平均成本将降至100美元以下。
这是一个良性循环的结果，更好、更便宜的电池扩大了市场，反过来又推动了投资，进一步提高了成本和性能。这一趋势意义重大，因为廉价电池对于将世界经济从煤炭和汽油等碳密集型能源转向至关重要。
电池和电动马达已经成为取代内燃机驱动的汽车的最有前途的技术。从历史上看，电池的高成本使得电动汽车比传统汽车贵得多。但一旦电池组变得足够便宜-专家估计非豪华车的价格约为每千瓦时100美元-电动汽车实际上应该会比同等的汽油动力汽车便宜。一旦考虑到电动汽车充电的低成本，成本优势将会更大，因此我们可以预期电池成本的下降将加速电动汽车的采用。
电池也是电力去碳化的关键技术。在过去的十年里，我们看到了太阳能和风能的强劲增长。但这些无碳能源有一个很大的缺点：它们只能在某些时候发电。当然，客户一直都在期待电力。
电池提供了一个显而易见的解决方案。但在这里，高昂的成本再次成为障碍。目前的价格仍然使使用电池在24小时内均匀分配白天太阳能的成本高得令人望而却步。相反，今天大多数公用事业规模的电池安装都是用来平滑供需的短期波动。缺乏存储限制了多少太阳能和风能可以有效地整合到电网中。
因此，未来十年电池价格的持续下降将是一个巨大的帮助，因为世界也在努力去碳化。
在过去的十年里，电池已经便宜了六倍，而且这一趋势看起来还会在较小程度上继续下去--但我们是如何走到这一步的呢？从增加的需求到智能电网解决方案，许多因素都是一个因素。也许最值得注意的是，特斯拉在整个过程中发挥了重要作用。特斯拉显然帮助普及了远程电池电动车的概念，但特斯拉既是一家汽车公司，也是一家电池公司。特斯拉比大多数其他公司更早意识到电池市场的潜在规模，并已成为电网存储市场的领头羊--这个市场有望在未来10年里变得更大。
很难获得电池真实成本的数据，因为市场上有太多的大公司参与了私下交易的谈判-通常会有很大的批量折扣。BloombergNEF是一家研究和咨询机构，与电池业务中的各种参与者都有关系。
BloombergNEF电池调查的主要作者詹姆斯·弗里思(James Frith)表示，我们利用我们在价值链所有部门的人脉。我们与终端用户以及制造商和该行业的其他工作人员都进行了交谈。我们与活跃在电动汽车领域的大多数大型汽车制造商以及中国、欧洲、美国和韩国的绝大多数一级电池制造商进行了交谈。
他们的数据显示，自2010年以来，电池成本下降了六倍以上。更便宜的电池扩大了市场，这有助于将成本推得更低。
这个过程甚至在2010年前就已经开始了。回想一下2003年，也就是特斯拉成立的那一年，当时蓬勃发展的笔记本电脑和手机市场正在迅速扩大锂离子电池市场。各公司正在投入数百万美元进行研发，以使电池更便宜、更强大。
JD·斯特劳贝尔(JD Straubel)是一名电气工程师，后来成为特斯拉的首席技术官。他意识到，这些电池已经足够好了-很快就会变得足够便宜-你可以用几千块电池为一辆电动汽车提供动力。他把这个想法带给了埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)，后者根据这个概念向特斯拉提供了早期资金。十年后，特斯拉和松下在内华达州建造了一家名为GigaFactory的大型电池工厂，旨在每年生产价值50GWh的电池组。这笔投资使特斯拉成为世界上最大的锂离子电池生产商和用户之一。
在特斯拉创立初期，电池仍然非常昂贵，因此只能销售电动跑车Roadster，这是一款标价6位数的电池电动跑车。但随着电池在成本和性能上的持续改善，特斯拉能够生产成本更低的Model S，然后是价格便宜得多的Model 3，消费者现在只需不到4万美元就可以买到。
施特劳贝尔在2014年表示，Model S是在Roadster推出大约五年后设计并推出的，我们看到电池技术、基础化学物质和电池组本身的包装方面有了大约40%的改进。这直接转化为我们如何才能在Model S中行驶近300英里，几乎85千瓦时的能量储存在一个实际上比跑车更小的背包中。
当然，今天人们购买的手机和笔记本电脑仍然比电动汽车多得多。但是，一辆电池电动车需要如此大的电池容量--40到100千瓦时，是智能手机的数千倍--以至于它们极大地增加了全球对锂离子电池的需求。这帮助推动了额外的价格下跌，这已经开始使使用电池来改善电网变得具有成本效益。
2017年，特斯拉在南澳大利亚的Hornsdale风电场完成了大规模电池系统的安装。它的存储容量为129兆瓦时-大约相当于2000部Model3或1000万部智能手机。该系统的所有者是法国可再生能源公司Neoen，该公司认为这是如此成功，以至于计划将其产能扩大50%。
电池的设计是以100兆瓦的速度提供能量。129兆瓦时的存储容量意味着它可以在一个小时多一点的时间内用完。但一小时的闲置产能可能非常有价值。为了确保不间断的服务，电力公司依赖于发电站--通常是燃气发电机--在压力大的时候，这些发电站可以在接到通知后立即启动。由于这些工厂一年可能只运行几个小时，每小时的成本可能会非常高。澳大利亚公用事业公司有时被迫在现货市场上向能够收取高达通常费率50倍的供应商购买电力。
霍恩斯代尔电池使该地区的电力公用事业公司在很大程度上选择退出这个现货市场。Neoen声称，电池已经为该地区的客户节省了数千万美元，同时使该地区的电网不太容易受到停电的影响。
在高峰负荷期间节省公用事业费用对公用事业公司来说是件好事，但对环境的好处可能不是非常显著。巨大的环境效益将在下一阶段出现，届时电网将有足够的电池储存一天中相当大一部分的能源需求。这将允许电网在白天储存相当于几个小时的太阳能，并在晚上将其放电-允许可再生能源提供大部分或最终所有的电力。如果有足够的容量，我们甚至可以跨季节储存能量，从夏季持有多余的太阳能，以保持电子在冬季流动。
那些有助于平衡短期波动的小型快速响应电池可能不是满足这些长期存储需求的最佳选择。还有许多其他电池技术正在开发中-最著名的是液流电池-可能更适合这种存储。而且，无论使用哪种电池技术，都必须与替代的储能方法竞争。其中一些，如抽水发电、压缩空气和蓄热，已经在有限的情况下使用。其他方面，如使用电力生产燃料，仍在开发中。
霍恩斯代尔电池可能是当时世界上最大的电池，但与美国的电力需求相比，它的容量仍然很小。美国每天消耗大约10太瓦时-是霍恩斯代尔工厂产能的7万多倍。随着电池成本的持续下降和可再生能源的激增，未来几年对公用事业规模的电池安装的需求只会增加。在2020年第一季度的收益报告中，特斯拉表示，它在世界各地的地点接到了多个订单，要建造比Hornsdale电池更大的电池系统。
note_en:
At today's "Battery Day" event, Tesla is expected to unveil new technologies that will drive the company's products over the next decade. Ahead of the event, we thought readers would enjoy this article, first published in May, about Tesla's role in the falling cost of batteries over the last decade.
  In 2010, a lithium-ion battery pack with 1 kWh of capacity—enough to power an electric car for three or four miles—cost more than $1,000. By 2019, the figure had fallen to $156, according to  data compiled by BloombergNEF. That's a massive drop, and experts expect continued—though perhaps not as rapid—progress in the coming decade. Several forecasters project the average cost of a kilowatt-hour of lithium-ion battery capacity to fall below $100 by the mid-2020s.
 That's the result of a virtuous circle where better, cheaper batteries expand the market, which in turn drives investments that produce further improvements in cost and performance. The trend is hugely significant because cheap batteries will be essential to shifting the world economy away from carbon-intensive energy sources like coal and gasoline.
 Batteries and electric motors have emerged as the most promising technology for replacing cars powered by internal combustion engines. The high cost of batteries has historically made electric cars much more expensive than conventional cars. But once battery packs get cheap enough—experts estimate around $100 per kWh for non-luxury vehicles—electric cars should actually become cheaper than equivalent gas-powered cars. The cost advantage will be even bigger once you factor in the low cost of charging an electric car, so we can expect falling battery costs to accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles.
 Batteries are a key technology for de-carbonizing electric power, too. We've seen robust growth of solar and wind energy sources over the last decade. But these carbon-free energy sources have a big downside: they only generate power some of the time. Customers, of course, expect power all the time.
 Batteries provide an obvious solution. But here again, high costs have been an obstacle. Current prices still make it cost-prohibitive to use batteries to distribute daytime solar energy evenly over 24 hours. Rather, most utility-scale battery installations today are used to smooth out shorter-term fluctuations in supply and demand. A lack of storage limits how much solar and wind energy can be usefully integrated into the electric grid.
 So continued declines in battery prices over the next decade will be a huge help as the world tries to de-carbonize, too.
 Batteries have already gotten six times cheaper within the last decade, and that trend looks to continue to a lesser extent moving forward—but how did we get here? A number of factors from increased demand to smart grid solutions have been a factor. And perhaps most notably, Tesla has played an important role throughout this process. Tesla obviously helped to popularize the concept of a long-range battery electric car, but Tesla has been a battery company as much as it is a car company. Tesla recognized the potential scale of the battery market before most other companies and has become a leading player in the market for grid storage—a market that's poised to become much bigger over the coming decade.
   Getting data on the true cost of batteries is difficult because so much of the market involves big companies negotiating private deals—often with substantial volume discounts. BloombergNEF is a research and consulting organization that has relationships with a wide variety of players in the battery business.
 "We leverage our contacts across all sectors of the value chain," said James Frith, the lead author of BloombergNEF's battery surveys. "We talk to both end users as well as manufacturers and other people working in the industry. We talk to most of the large auto makers who are active in the electric vehicle space and the majority of the tier 1 battery manufacturers in China, Europe, the US, and South Korea."
 Their data shows battery costs falling more than sixfold since 2010. Cheaper batteries have expanded the market, which has helped to drive costs even lower.
 This process was going on even before 2010. Think back to 2003, the year Tesla was founded. At the time, a booming market for laptops and cell phones was rapidly expanding the market for lithium-ion batteries. Companies were pouring millions of dollars into research and development to make batteries cheaper and more powerful.
 JD Straubel, an electrical engineer who went on to become the CTO of Tesla, realized that these batteries had gotten good enough—and would soon be cheap enough—that you could use a few thousand of them to power an electric car. He brought the idea to Elon Musk, who provided early funding to Tesla based on the concept. A decade later, Tesla and Panasonic built a massive battery factory in Nevada called the Gigafactory that was designed to produce 50 GWh worth of battery packs annually. The investment made Tesla one of the world's biggest producers—and users—of lithium-ion batteries.
 In Tesla's early days, batteries were still so expensive that it was only viable to sell the Roadster, a battery electric sports car with a six-figure price tag. But as batteries continued improving in cost and performance, Tesla was able to build the lower-cost Model S and then the much cheaper Model 3, which consumers can now buy for less than $40,000.
 "Model S was designed and introduced about five years after the Roadster, and we saw improvements of around 40 percent on the battery technology, the fundamental chemistry, the packaging of the battery pack itself,"  Straubel said in 2014. "That directly translated into how we can get close to 300 miles of range in a Model S, almost 85 kWh of energy storage in a pack that's actually smaller than the Roadster pack."
 Today, of course, people still buy many more cell phones and laptops than electric cars. But a battery electric car needs so much battery capacity—40 to 100 kWh, thousands of times more than a smartphone—that they've significantly increased the global demand for lithium-ion batteries. That has helped drive additional price declines, which have started to make it cost-effective to use batteries to improve the electric grid.
   In 2017 Tesla  completed installation of a massive battery system at the Hornsdale Wind Farm in South Australia. Its storage capacity was 129 MWh—about as much as 2,000 Model 3s or 10 million smartphones. The system's owner, the French renewable company Neoen,  considers it such a success that it plans to  expand its capacity by 50 percent.
 The battery is designed to deliver energy at a rate of 100 MW. With 129 MWh of storage capacity, that means that it can run out in a little more than an hour. But an hour of spare capacity can be  hugely valuable. To ensure uninterrupted service, electric utilities rely on "peaker plants"—often gas-fired generators—that can be powered up at a moment's notice during times of stress. Because these plants may only run for a few hours a year, the per-hour cost can be very high. Australian utilities have sometimes been forced to buy power on the spot market from suppliers who are able to  charge as much as 50 times the usual rate.
 The Hornsdale battery has allowed the region's electric utility to largely opt out of this spot market.  Neoen claims that the battery has already saved customers in the region tens of millions of dollars, while simultaneously making the region's grid less susceptible to blackouts.
 Saving utilities money during peak loads is nice for the utilities, but the environmental benefits may not be very significant. The big environmental benefits will come in the next phase, when the electric grid has enough batteries to store a significant fraction of the day's energy demand. That will allow the grid to store up several hours worth of solar energy during the daytime and discharge it at night—allowing renewable energy to supply most or eventually all of the electricity. With enough capacity, we could even store energy across seasons, holding an excess of solar power from the summer to keep electrons flowing during the winter.
 The sorts of small, fast-response batteries that help balance out short-term fluctuations may not be the best option for these longer-term storage needs. There are a number of other battery technologies in development—most notably  flow batteries—that may be better-suited to this sort of storage. And whatever battery technology that is used will have to compete with alternate energy storage methods. Some of those, like pumped hydro, compressed air, and heat storage, are already in use in limited cases. Others, like using electricity for the production of fuels, are still in development.
 The Hornsdale battery might have been the biggest in the world at the time, but its capacity is still tiny compared to US electricity demand. The US consumes roughly 10 terawatt-hours each day—more than 70,000 times the capacity of the Hornsdale plant. With battery costs continuing to fall and renewable power sources proliferating, demand for utility-scale battery installations will only increase in the coming years. In its  earnings report for the first quarter of 2020, Tesla said it had multiple orders to build battery systems even bigger than the Hornsdale battery at sites around the world.
title: 从智能手机到特斯拉，更便宜的电池的故事
title_en: The story of cheaper batteries, from smartphones to Teslas

note:
巴西最大的两家金融服务机构Bradesco和ItaúUnibanco试图包抄来自Neo银行和其他潜在挑战者的竞争，它们投资了Quanto公司，这是一家开发技术的公司，让零售商和其他企业能够获得金融信息和服务。
加入巴西两大银行行列的还有拉丁美洲最大的风险投资公司之一卡泽克风险投资公司(Kaszek Ventures)和数十亿美元的对冲基金Coatue。
根据一份声明，Bradesco通过其InovaBra Ventures投资基金加入了这轮融资，而Itaú则直接投资，并获得了巴西央行的批准。
Quanto首席执行官理查德·塔维拉(Richard Taveira)在一份声明中表示：“开放银行改变了我们理解和消费金融服务的方式，但看到巴西市场以如此积极的方式拥抱这一新时刻，令人相当兴奋。”“巴西有潜力引领全球开放银行业务的使用，本轮融资就证明了这一点.”
巴西中央银行深度投资于开放银行监管的前景，以允许支付处理商与金融服务价值链中的技术提供商、零售商和其他服务提供商之间共享信息和数据。
Quanto，它提供标准化的银行数据应用程序编程接口，允许机构大幅缩短访问银行账户数据所需的时间。
该公司表示，Quanto技术可以通过与B2B服务相同的API平台进行金融产品分销。Quanto声称，其服务将使客户更容易通过单点登录模式获得低息信贷额度，并通过在单一流程中与多家贷款人共享银行数据来获得具有竞争力的利率。
Taveira说：“Quanto为银行和金融技术公司提供了竞争的渠道，消费者才是赢家。”
note_en:
Trying to outflank competition from neo banks and other potential challengers, two of Brazil’s largest financial services institutions, Bradesco and Itaú Unibanco, have invested in  Quanto, a company developing technology to let retailers and other businesses access financial information and services.
 Joining Brazil’s two largest banks are Kaszek Ventures, one of Latin America’s largest venture capital firms, and Coatue, the multi-billion dollar hedge fund.
 Bradesco joined the round through its InovaBra Ventures investment fund while Itaú invested directly and had its participation approved by Brazil’s Central  Bank,   according to a statement.
  “Open banking changes the way we understand and consume financial services, but it’s quite exciting to see the Brazilian market embracing this new moment in such a positive way,” said Richard Taveira, Quanto’s chief executive, in a statement. “Brazil has the potential to lead the use of open banking worldwide, and this round is a testament to that.”
 Brazil’s Central Bank  is deeply invested in the prospect of opening up banking regulation to allow information and data sharing between payment processors and technology providers, retailers, and other service providers in the financial services value chain.
 Quanto, which provides standardized bank data application programming interfaces that allow institutions to slash the time it takes to ccess bank account data.
 The Quanto technology could enable financial product distribution through the same  API   platform as business to business services, the company said. Quanto claims that its services will make it easier for customers to access low-interest credit lines with a single sign-on model and to receive competitive interest rates by sharing banking data with multiple lenders in a single flow.
 “Quanto provides the rail for banks and fintechs to compete, and consumers are the ones who win”, said Taveira.
title: 巴西银行试图通过投资1500万美元收购Quanto来包抄挑战者
title_en: Brazil’s banks try to outflank challengers by investing in a $15 million round for Quanto
